result:
Confirmed: `struct dl_list` is defined only once, and there is no `src/utils/list.c` file anywhere in the repo — this is a header-only doubly-linked list implementation.

## `struct dl_list` definition
`src/utils/list.h:15-18`
```c
struct dl_list {
    struct dl_list *next;
    struct dl_list *prev;
};
```
A minimal intrusive doubly-linked-list node (Linux-kernel-style, embedded inside a containing struct rather than holding data itself).

## Operating functions — all in `list.h`, none in `list.c`

`src/utils/list.c` **does not exist** (confirmed via `find`, and via `compile_commands.json`, which contains no `list.c` entry — the only `*list.c` files in the build are `ap_list.c` and `blacklist.c`, unrelated). Every operation on `struct dl_list` is implemented as a `static inline` function or macro directly in the header, verified via clangd's `documentSymbol` on `list.h`:

**Inline functions** (real symbols, indexed by clangd):
- `dl_list_init(list)` — line 22: initializes list to point to itself (empty circular list)
- `dl_list_add(list, item)` — line 28: inserts `item` right after `list` (head insert)
- `dl_list_add_tail(list, item)` — line 36: inserts `item` at the tail, via `dl_list_add(list->prev, item)`
- `dl_list_del(item)` — line 41: unlinks `item` from its list, then nulls its `next`/`prev`
- `dl_list_empty(list)` — line 49: returns true if `list->next == list`
- `dl_list_len(list)` — line 54: walks the list counting nodes (iteration)

**Macros** (not seen by `documentSymbol`, expand to iteration/accessor logic, verified by reading the file directly):
- `dl_list_entry(item, type, member)` — line 67: container-of style cast from list node back to enclosing struct
- `dl_list_first(list, type, member)` / `dl_list_last(...)` — lines 70/74: get first/last entry (or NULL if empty)
- `dl_list_for_each(item, list, type, member)` — line 78: forward iteration
- `dl_list_for_each_safe(item, n, list, type, member)` — line 83: forward iteration safe against removal of the current item
- `dl_list_for_each_reverse(item, list, type, member)` — line 89: backward iteration
- `DEFINE_DL_LIST(name)` — line 94: static-initializes a self-referencing empty list

No `.c` file provides any of these — the entire API is compiled inline wherever `list.h` is included.
